Basilischi.description: The Basilischi is an organized crime group based in Basilicata, Italy, officially formed in 1994 by Giovanni Luigi Cosentino in Potenza. Its strongholds include Policoro, Montalbano Jonico, Pisticci, Scanzano Jonico, and the Val d'Agri and Vulture regions. A maxi-trial in 1999 led to convictions of key members, causing the group to fracture and fall under the influence of the 'Ndrangheta.

Trimboli 'ndrina.description: The Trimboli are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Platì, Italy. They have been active in Volpiano, Turin since the 1970s, allied with the Marando and Agresta clans, and in Australia since the 1960s. Involved in international drug trafficking, they have faced multiple arrests and operations against them, including Operation Igres and Revenge 2. The clan remains active, with members still being arrested.

Redd Alert.description: Indigenous Canadian criminal gang operating as both a prison gang and a street gang, founded in 1999 by Robert Lee Wagner and other First Nations inmates in Alberta. Based in Western Canada, it is involved in drug trafficking, assault, murder, and other crimes. The gang maintains alliances with the Hells Angels and other organized crime groups.

'Ndrina Maio.description: The Maio are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Martino di Taurianova, Italy, involved in drug trafficking, usury, and extortion. They have been subject to law enforcement operations, including the 2011 'Tutto in famiglia' operation that led to 21 arrests and asset seizures.

'Ndrina Marando.description: The Marando 'ndrina is a Calabrian 'Ndrangheta clan based in Platì, Italy. Active in northern Italy, particularly in Milan, Volpiano, Turin, and Liguria, it is involved in cocaine and heroin trafficking. The clan has been engaged in feuds with rival groups such as the Stefanelli family. Key figures include Francesco Marando, who was killed in 1996, and Pasquale Marando, who carried out revenge killings.

Giglio.description: The Giglio are a 'ndrina (criminal clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Strongoli, Calabria, Italy. They are active in international drug trafficking, usury, arms trafficking, money laundering, and are considered the heads of the Locale of Strongoli. The clan has been involved in a feud with the Valente clan and was targeted in the 2018 Operation Stige, which led to numerous arrests.

Jemaah Islamiyah.description: Southeast Asian Islamist militant group based in Indonesia, founded in 1993 by Abu Bakar Bashir and Abdullah Sungkar. Dedicated to establishing an Islamic state in Southeast Asia, it perpetrated the 2002 Bali bombings and other attacks. Designated a terrorist group by the UN and multiple countries. The group was active until 2024.

Nuova Famiglia.description: The Nuova Famiglia was an Italian Camorra confederation created in the late 1970s to oppose Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata. Headed by powerful Camorra bosses including Carmine Alfieri, the Nuvoletta brothers, Michele Zaza, Luigi Giuliano, and Antonio Bardellino, it was allied with the Sicilian Mafia. The organization was active primarily in Campania and other Italian cities, with a presence in South America, and engaged in various criminal activities. It became defunct in the 1990s.

White Boy Posse.description: Canadian white supremacist neo-Nazi organized crime group founded in 2003 in Edmonton, Alberta. Primarily active in Western Canada, involved in drug trafficking, assault, and murder. Members often come from impoverished backgrounds and display Nazi symbolism. The group has ties to the Hells Angels and conducts cross-border criminal activities.

edges.evidence_quote: Additionally, the club has also formed alliances with various street gangs, including the Independent Soldiers, the Red Scorpions, the United Nations and the White Boy Posse, and smaller motorcycle gangs, such as Bacchus, the Gate Keepers and the Red Devils.

'Ndrina Bruni.description: A 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. Involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and infiltration of businesses. Key members include Michele Bruni (arrested 2009) and Luca Bruni (disappeared 2012). The clan has been targeted in major police operations, including a 2010 arrest of 49 people.

Chichesters.description: The Chichesters were an early 19th-century Irish-American street gang based in the Five Points neighborhood of New York City. Founded by John Chichester in the 1820s, they engaged in theft, illegal gambling, and robbery. The gang allied with the Dead Rabbits against the Bowery Boys and was eventually absorbed by the Whyos after the American Civil War.
